questions.text: When was Muslim League formed?
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The All-India Muslim League was formed in 1906 in Dhaka, which was then part of British India and is now the capital of Bangladesh. It was founded as a political party representing the interests of Muslims in India.

questions.text: When was the Durand Line demarcated?
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The Durand Line, the border between Afghanistan and Pakistan, was demarcated in 1893 by Sir Mortimer Durand, a British diplomat, during the rule of the British Empire in India.

questions.text: The philosopher who gave the idea of Asabia was:
questions.subject: Pakistan Studies

options.explanation: The philosopher who gave the idea of Asabiya (also spelled Assabiya) was:Ibn KhaldunIbn Khaldun (1332-1406) was a prominent Islamic scholar and thinker who made significant contributions to the fields of history, sociology, and political philosophy. His concept of Asabiya is a key element of his theories on social cohesion and the rise and fall of dynasties.
